insight.summary: Stripe API returns HTTP 200 with an error body when a request is rate-limited
insight.detail: When rate-limited, Stripe does not return a 429. Instead it returns a 200 response whose JSON body contains an error object with type 'invalid_request_error' and code 'rate_limit'. Checking only the HTTP status code will cause rate-limit errors to be silently ignored and treated as success.
insight.action: Always parse the Stripe response body for an 'error' field regardless of the HTTP status code, and raise on any non-null error object.

insight.summary: GitHub Search API returns 422 when query strings contain unquoted special characters such as colons or slashes
insight.detail: The GitHub Search API parses query strings using its own qualifier syntax. Characters like ':', '/', and '.' have special meaning. Passing user-supplied strings without quoting them causes a 422 Unprocessable Entity with the message 'Validation Failed'. This is not documented clearly in the API reference.
insight.action: Wrap free-text search terms in double quotes when constructing GitHub Search API queries, e.g. q='"my search term" in:file'.

insight.summary: AWS S3 ListObjectsV2 silently truncates results at 1000 objects
insight.detail: S3 ListObjectsV2 returns at most 1000 keys per call. If the bucket contains more objects, the response sets IsTruncated=true and provides a NextContinuationToken. Code that does not check IsTruncated will silently miss objects beyond the first 1000 with no error or warning.
insight.action: Use the AWS SDK paginator (e.g. get_paginator('list_objects_v2') in boto3) rather than calling ListObjectsV2 directly, to ensure all objects are returned.

insight.summary: The GITHUB_TOKEN in GitHub Actions cannot trigger other workflow runs
insight.detail: Workflows triggered by a GITHUB_TOKEN-authenticated push or tag creation do not fire other workflow triggers. This is a deliberate GitHub restriction to prevent recursive workflow loops. PATs or GitHub App tokens are required to trigger downstream workflows from within a workflow.
insight.action: Use a Personal Access Token (PAT) or a GitHub App installation token stored as a repository secret when a workflow needs to push commits or tags that should trigger other workflows.

insight.summary: Python requests changes POST to GET when following a 301 or 302 redirect
insight.detail: RFC 2616 allows clients to change the method from POST to GET on 301/302 redirects, and requests follows this behaviour. A POST to an endpoint that redirects will silently become a GET, dropping the request body. This can result in data not being submitted without any error being raised.
insight.action: Either disable redirect following with allow_redirects=False and handle redirects manually, or use a 307/308 redirect on the server side which preserves the method. Check the final response URL to detect unexpected redirects.

insight.summary: datetime.utcnow() returns a naive datetime with no timezone info and is deprecated in Python 3.12
insight.detail: datetime.utcnow() returns a datetime object with no tzinfo, making it indistinguishable from a local time in arithmetic and comparisons. Python 3.12 deprecated it with a DeprecationWarning. Code using utcnow() mixed with timezone-aware datetimes raises TypeError.
insight.action: Replace datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(UTC) where UTC is imported from datetime (from datetime import UTC). The result is timezone-aware and works correctly in comparisons and arithmetic.

insight.summary: Pydantic v2 removed model.dict() and model.json(); use model.model_dump() and model.model_dump_json()
insight.detail: Pydantic v2 is a near-complete rewrite with a breaking API. The dict() and json() instance methods were removed. In v2, use model.model_dump() and model.model_dump_json(). Additionally, validators use @field_validator instead of @validator, and model_config replaces the inner Config class. Importing from pydantic.v1 is available as a compatibility shim but should not be used in new code.
insight.action: When migrating to Pydantic v2, replace .dict() with .model_dump(), .json() with .model_dump_json(), and @validator with @field_validator. Run pydantic's migration script (bump-pydantic) to automate the bulk of changes.

insight.summary: npm ci fails if package-lock.json is absent or out of sync with package.json
insight.detail: Unlike npm install, npm ci requires a package-lock.json to exist and will exit with an error if package.json dependencies do not exactly match the lockfile. It also deletes node_modules before installing. This makes CI builds reproducible but means any uncommitted lockfile change will break the build.
insight.action: Always commit package-lock.json to the repository. After adding or updating packages locally with npm install, commit the updated lockfile before pushing. Use npm ci in CI environments and npm install only for local development.

insight.summary: GitHub Actions secrets are not available in workflows triggered by pull requests from forks
insight.detail: For security, GitHub does not pass repository secrets to workflow runs triggered by pull_request events from forked repositories. The secrets context is empty. This affects any CI step that needs an API key, deployment credential, or registry password. The workflow runs but silently uses empty strings, which can cause confusing authentication failures.
insight.action: Use pull_request_target instead of pull_request only when you understand the security implications (it runs in the context of the base repo and has access to secrets). For safer secret access from forks, use environment protection rules with manual approval. Avoid requiring secrets for basic CI steps like unit tests.

insight.summary: Placing COPY . . before dependency installation in a Dockerfile invalidates the layer cache on every code change
insight.detail: Docker rebuilds all layers from the first changed layer onwards. If source code is copied before dependencies are installed, any code change triggers a full dependency reinstall. Since code changes far more often than dependency specs, this makes builds unnecessarily slow.
insight.action: Copy only the dependency manifest files (requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, package.json, etc.) first and install dependencies, then copy the rest of the source code. This way the dependency layer is cached unless the manifest changes.

insight.summary: rust-toolchain.toml is silently ignored when a GitHub Actions matrix sets the toolchain via dtolnay/rust-toolchain
insight.detail: When a workflow uses a matrix to test multiple Rust toolchain versions and passes the toolchain version as an input to dtolnay/rust-toolchain, that action overrides rust-toolchain.toml. The file is not read. This means the matrix-specified toolchain is used, not the pinned version in the file, leading to version inconsistencies between local and CI builds.
insight.action: Choose a single source of truth for the toolchain: either use rust-toolchain.toml and remove the toolchain input from the action, or remove rust-toolchain.toml and manage all toolchain selection via the matrix. Do not use both simultaneously.

insight.summary: actions/checkout fetches only a single commit by default, breaking tag-based version detection
insight.detail: By default, actions/checkout performs a shallow clone (fetch-depth: 1). Tools that compute versions from git tags (e.g. setuptools-scm, dunamai, git describe) cannot see any tags in a shallow clone and fall back to a default or raise an error. This produces incorrect version strings in release artefacts.
insight.action: Set fetch-depth: 0 in the actions/checkout step to fetch the full history and all tags when the build needs git-based version detection.

insight.summary: FastAPI BackgroundTasks run after the response has been sent; the request context is no longer available
insight.detail: Functions added via BackgroundTasks run in the same event loop but after the response is returned to the client. Any dependencies injected via Depends() that are scoped to the request have already been cleaned up. Attempting to use a request-scoped database session or auth context in a background task will raise errors or produce undefined behaviour.
insight.action: Do not pass request-scoped dependencies into background tasks. Instead pass only the data needed (e.g. IDs, plain values) and acquire new resources (database connections, sessions) inside the background function itself.

insight.summary: SQLite requires WAL journal mode for concurrent read access alongside writes
insight.detail: In the default DELETE journal mode, SQLite acquires an exclusive lock during writes, blocking all readers. WAL (Write-Ahead Logging) mode allows concurrent reads during a write by separating the write-ahead log from the main database file. Without WAL, multi-threaded or multi-process applications see 'database is locked' errors under write load.
insight.action: Enable WAL mode immediately after opening any SQLite connection used in a concurrent context: PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL. Pair with PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL for a good balance of safety and performance.

insight.summary: Passing an async function directly to useEffect silently swallows the cleanup return value
insight.detail: React expects useEffect to return either undefined or a synchronous cleanup function. An async function returns a Promise, not undefined or a function. React ignores the Promise, so cleanup never runs. This causes memory leaks, state updates on unmounted components, and missed cancellations of in-flight requests.
insight.action: Never pass an async function directly to useEffect. Instead, define the async logic inside the effect and call it: useEffect(() => { const fetchData = async () => { ... }; fetchData(); return () => { /* cleanup */ }; }, []).

insight.summary: AWS Lambda cold start latency can be reduced by keeping functions warm with scheduled EventBridge pings
insight.detail: Lambda functions that are not invoked for several minutes are de-provisioned, causing the next invocation to incur a cold start. A common workaround was to schedule CloudWatch Events/EventBridge rules to invoke the function every 5 minutes to keep execution environments alive.
insight.action: Create an EventBridge scheduled rule to invoke the Lambda function every 5 minutes with a keep-warm payload, and return early without executing business logic when this payload is detected.

insight.summary: Use pip install --user when system Python is read-only to install packages without root
insight.detail: On systems where the system Python site-packages directory is not writable, pip install fails with a permission error. The --user flag installs packages into ~/.local/lib/pythonX.Y/site-packages, which is always user-writable.
insight.action: Run pip install --user <package> when installing packages on a system Python without root access.
